prompt: Use print to output the exact text: Hello, World!

learningTip: print() is how a program communicates a result to the outside world.

title: Add Two Numbers
prompt: Read two whole numbers (each on its own line) and print their sum using arithmetic addition.
hints: Numbers typed by a user arrive as text and must be converted before doing math.
Read line 1 into a, read line 2 into b, then print a + b.

a = int(input())
b = int(input())
print(a + b)
learningTip: input() always returns a string; wrap it in int() to do arithmetic.

title: Store and Reuse a Variable
prompt: Read a person's name, store it in a variable, then print: Hello, <name>!
hints: A variable is a labeled box that remembers a value so you can use it later.
Store input() in a variable called name, then build the greeting string from it.

name = input()
print("Hello, " + name + "!")
learningTip: Variables let you name data so you can reuse it instead of repeating yourself.

title: Even or Odd
prompt: Read a whole number and print "even" if it is divisible by 2, otherwise print "odd". Use an if/else condition.
hints: A number is even when the remainder of dividing it by 2 is zero.
Compute n % 2; if it equals 0 print even, otherwise print odd.
learningTip: if/else lets your program choose between paths based on a condition.

title: Sum From One To N
prompt: Read a whole number N and use a loop to add up every integer from 1 to N, then print the total.
hints: Adding 1 + 2 + ... + N means repeating an addition many times — a job for a loop.
Start total at 0, loop i from 1 through N adding i each pass, then print total.

n = int(input())
total = 0
for i in range(1, n + 1):
    total += i
print(total)
learningTip: A for loop repeats a block once per item in a range or collection.

title: Sum a List of Numbers
prompt: Read one line of space-separated numbers into a list and print the sum of all elements in the list.
hints: A list holds many values together; splitting a line of text gives a list of pieces.
Split the input on spaces, convert each piece to int to form a list, then sum the list.

nums = [int(x) for x in input().split()]
print(sum(nums))
learningTip: Lists store ordered collections; split() turns a line into a list of tokens.

title: Write a Square Function
prompt: Define a function that returns the square of its parameter, then read a number, call the function, and print the returned value.
hints: A function is a reusable named recipe that takes input (parameters) and gives back a result.
